---
title: "lothian_cluster"
author: "Guanyu"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
fig.align = "center", fig.width=7, fig.height=9, dpi = 300
)
```

```{r load-lib}
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(factoextra)
library(cluster)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggh4x)
library(scales)
library(e1071) # for cmeans
library(mclust) # for ARI & finite mixtures
library(aricode) # for NMI
library(salso) # for VI
library(patchwork)
```

```{r cluster helper function, echo = FALSE}
cluster_palette <- c(
  "1" = "#1f78b4", 
  "2" = "#e31a1c", 
  "3" = "#33a02c", 
  "4" = "#ff7f00", 
  "5" = "#6a3d9a"   
)

# --- Robust alignment function (Hungarian algorithm) ---
align_by_overlap <- function(ref, alt) {
  stopifnot(length(ref) == length(alt))
  ref <- factor(ref)
  alt <- factor(alt)

  tab <- table(ref, alt)
  keep_rows <- rowSums(tab) > 0
  keep_cols <- colSums(tab) > 0
  tab2 <- tab[keep_rows, keep_cols, drop = FALSE]

  if (!requireNamespace("clue", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Package 'clue' required. Install via install.packages('clue').")
  }

  assign <- clue::solve_LSAP(tab2, maximum = TRUE)

  row_lab <- rownames(tab2)
  col_lab <- colnames(tab2)
  mapping_ref_to_alt <- setNames(col_lab[assign], row_lab)
  map_alt_to_ref <- setNames(names(mapping_ref_to_alt), mapping_ref_to_alt)

  all_alt_levels <- levels(alt)
  if (length(setdiff(all_alt_levels, names(map_alt_to_ref))) > 0) {
    leftovers <- setdiff(all_alt_levels, names(map_alt_to_ref))
    nearest <- apply(tab[, leftovers, drop = FALSE], 2, function(x) {
      if (sum(x) == 0) rownames(tab)[1] else rownames(tab)[which.max(x)]
    })
    map_alt_to_ref <- c(map_alt_to_ref, nearest)
  }

  aligned <- factor(map_alt_to_ref[as.character(alt)], levels = levels(ref))
  aligned
}

plot_cluster_boxplot <- function(data, indicator_cols, cluster_col, title_text) {

  long_df <- data %>%
    select(all_of(indicator_cols), cluster = {{ cluster_col }}) %>%
    pivot_longer(
      cols = all_of(indicator_cols),
      names_to = "Indicator",
      values_to = "value"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Indicator = factor(Indicator, levels = indicator_cols),
      cluster   = factor(cluster),
      cluster   = factor(cluster, levels = sort(unique(cluster)))  # 1,2,3...
    )

  # restrict palette to actually used cluster levels but keep order
  pal_used <- cluster_palette[levels(long_df$cluster)]

  ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Indicator, y = value, fill = cluster)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.6, alpha = 0.9) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = pal_used, name = "Cluster", drop = FALSE) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "Indicator",
      y = "Scaled value"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title  = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 9),
      legend.position = "top"
    )
}

similarity_metrics <- function(a, b) {
  a <- as.factor(a)
  b <- as.factor(b)
  tibble(
    ARI = adjustedRandIndex(a, b),
    NMI = aricode::NMI(a, b),
    VI = salso::VI(a, b))
}

make_cluster_colors <- function(cluster_levels) {

  base_colors <- c(
    "1" = "#1f78b4",
    "2" = "#33a02c", 
    "3" = "#e31a1c", 
    "4" = "#ff7f00", 
    "5" = "#6a3d9a"   
  )
  
  colors <- base_colors[cluster_levels]
  
  # Add NA if present
  if ("NA" %in% cluster_levels) {
    colors["NA"] <- "grey60"
  }
  
  return(colors)
}

plot_cluster_map <- function(sf_data, cluster_col, title_text) {

  cluster_vec <- sf_data[[cluster_col]]

  # Count clusters including NA
  count_df <- count_clusters(cluster_vec)
  
  # Extract ordered factor levels
  cluster_levels <- as.character(count_df$cluster)
  
  # Legend labels + colors
  labels <- make_cluster_labels(count_df)
  colors <- make_cluster_colors(cluster_levels)

  ggplot(sf_data) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = forcats::fct_explicit_na(as.factor(.data[[cluster_col]]),
                                               na_level = "NA")),
            color = NA) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = colors, labels = labels, name = "Cluster") +
    labs(title = title_text) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
}

difference_map <- function(data, clusterA, clusterB, title = "Difference Map") {

  # 1. Create categorical difference variable
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(
      diff_cat = case_when(
        is.na(.data[[clusterA]]) | is.na(.data[[clusterB]]) ~ "NA",
        .data[[clusterA]] == .data[[clusterB]] ~ "Same",
        TRUE ~ "Different"
      )
    )

  # 2. Count categories
  n_same <- sum(data$diff_cat == "Same", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_diff <- sum(data$diff_cat == "Different", na.rm = TRUE)
  n_na   <- sum(data$diff_cat == "NA", na.rm = TRUE)

  # 3. Build legend labels
  diff_labels <- c(
    sprintf("Same (n = %d)", n_same),
    sprintf("Different (n = %d)", n_diff),
    sprintf("NA (n = %d)", n_na)
  )

  # 4. Order factor levels
  data <- data %>%
    mutate(diff_cat = factor(diff_cat, levels = c("Same", "Different", "NA")))

  # 5. Produce map
  p <- ggplot(data) +
    geom_sf(aes(fill = diff_cat), color = NA) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      name   = "Different cluster",
      values = c(
        "Same"      = "#d3d3d3",  # light grey
        "Different" = "#e31a1c",  # red
        "NA"        = "#7f7f7f"   # dark grey
      ),
      labels = diff_labels
    ) +
    labs(title = title) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      legend.position = "right"
    )

  return(p)
}
```

```{r load data}
simd <- readr::read_csv("simd2020_withgeog/simd2020_withinds.csv")
simd_shp <- sf::st_read("simd2020_withgeog/sc_dz_11.shp")
simd_joined <- simd_shp %>%
  left_join(simd, by = c("DataZone" = "Data_Zone"))
joint_board_lookup <- tribble(
  ~Council_area,                          ~Joint_Board_area,
  "East Ayrshire",                       "Ayrshire",
  "North Ayrshire",                      "Ayrshire",
  "South Ayrshire",                      "Ayrshire",
  "Scottish Borders",                    "Borders",
  "Clackmannanshire",                    "Central Scotland",
  "Falkirk",                             "Central Scotland",
  "Stirling",                            "Central Scotland",
  "Dumfries and Galloway",               "Dumfries and Galloway",
  "Argyll and Bute",                     "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "East Dunbartonshire",                 "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "West Dunbartonshire",                 "Dunbartonshire and Argyll & Bute",
  "Fife",                                "Fife",
  "Aberdeen City",                       "Grampian",
  "Aberdeenshire",                       "Grampian",
  "Moray",                               "Grampian",
  "Glasgow City",                        "Glasgow",
  "Highland",                            "Highlands and Islands",
  "Na h-Eileanan an Iar",                "Highlands and Islands",
  "North Lanarkshire",                   "Lanarkshire",
  "South Lanarkshire",                   "Lanarkshire",
  "East Lothian",                        "Lothian",
  "City of Edinburgh",                   "Lothian",
  "Midlothian",                          "Lothian",
  "West Lothian",                        "Lothian",
  "Orkney Islands",                      "Orkney and Shetland",
  "Shetland Islands",                    "Orkney and Shetland",
  "East Renfrewshire",                   "Renfrewshire",
  "Inverclyde",                          "Renfrewshire",
  "Renfrewshire",                        "Renfrewshire",
  "Angus",                               "Tayside",
  "Dundee City",                         "Tayside",
  "Perth and Kinross",                   "Tayside"
)

simd_joined <- simd_joined %>%
  left_join(joint_board_lookup, by = c("Council_area" = "Council_area"))
```

```{r}
# Pull out Lothian data

indicator_cols <- c(
"income_rate", "income_count",
"employment_rate", "employment_count",
"CIF", "ALCOHOL", "DRUG", "SMR", "DEPRESS",
"LBWT", "EMERG", "Attendance", "Attainment",
"no_qualifications", "not_participating", "University",
"crime_count", "crime_rate",
"overcrowded_count", "nocentralheating_count",
"overcrowded_rate", "nocentralheating_rate",
"drive_petrol", "drive_GP", "drive_post",
"drive_primary", "drive_retail", "drive_secondary",
"PT_GP", "PT_post", "PT_retail", "broadband"
)

simd_lothian_raw <- simd_joined %>%
  filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(DataZone, any_of(indicator_cols)) %>%
  mutate(
    across(
      where(is.character) & !matches("^DataZone$"), 
      ~ as.numeric(str_remove(.x, "%"))
      )
    )
```

```{r}
# Summary for missing data

na_summary <- simd_lothian_raw %>%
  summarise(
    across(
      all_of(indicator_cols),
      list(
        Missing_Count   = ~ sum(is.na(.)),
        Percent_Missing = ~ mean(is.na(.)) * 100
        ),
      .names = "{.col}.{.fn}")
    ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    everything(),
    names_to  = c("Indicator", ".value"),
    names_sep = "\\.") %>%
  arrange(desc(Missing_Count))

kable(na_summary %>% filter(Missing_Count > 0) , 
      digits = 2,
      caption = "Indicators with Missing Values in Lothian") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# Keep indicators with complete data

lothian_ind <- na_summary %>%
  filter(Percent_Missing <= 10) %>%
  pull(Indicator)

simd_lothian <- simd_lothian_raw %>%
  select(DataZone, all_of(lothian_ind)) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(lothian_ind))

# Standardize for PCA & clustering

lothian_data <- simd_lothian %>%
  select(-DataZone) %>%
  scale() %>%
  as.data.frame()
```

```{r}
# Domain Mapping

indicator_domains <- tribble(
  ~Indicator,                ~Domain,
  # Income
  "income_rate",             "Income",
  "income_count",            "Income",

  # Employment
  "employment_rate",         "Employment",
  "employment_count",        "Employment",

  # Health
  "CIF",                     "Health",
  "ALCOHOL",                 "Health",
  "DRUG",                    "Health",
  "SMR",                     "Health",
  "DEPRESS",                 "Health",
  "LBWT",                    "Health",
  "EMERG",                   "Health",

  # Education
  "Attendance",              "Education",
  "Attainment",              "Education",
  "no_qualifications",       "Education",
  "not_participating",       "Education",
  "University",              "Education",

  # Access
  "drive_petrol",            "Access",
  "drive_GP",                "Access",
  "drive_post",              "Access",
  "drive_primary",           "Access",
  "drive_retail",            "Access",
  "drive_secondary",         "Access",
  "PT_GP",                   "Access",
  "PT_post",                 "Access",
  "PT_retail",               "Access",
  "broadband",               "Access",

  # Crime
  "crime_count",             "Crime",
  "crime_rate",              "Crime",

  # Housing
  "overcrowded_count",       "Housing",
  "nocentralheating_count",  "Housing",
  "overcrowded_rate",        "Housing",
  "nocentralheating_rate",   "Housing"
)

domain_vars <- indicator_domains %>%
  filter(Indicator %in% colnames(lothian_data)) %>%
  group_by(Domain) %>%
  summarise(vars = list(Indicator)) %>%
  deframe()

library(FactoMineR)
library(factoextra)

# Run PCA for each domain and store the PCA object
pca_results <- lapply(names(domain_vars), function(domain) {
  vars <- domain_vars[[domain]]
  
  # Keep only variables present in the dataset
  valid_vars <- intersect(vars, colnames(lothian_data))
  
  if (length(valid_vars) == 0) {
    message(paste("Skipping domain:", domain, "(no valid variables)"))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # --- 1. Run PCA ---
  pca <- prcomp(lothian_data[, valid_vars, drop = FALSE], scale. = TRUE)
  
  # --- 2. Compute variance explained ---
  var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)
  var_df <- tibble(
    PC = factor(paste0("PC", 1:length(var_explained)), 
                levels = paste0("PC", 1:length(var_explained))),
    Variance = var_explained
  )
  
    # --- 3. Create visualization ---
  plot <- ggplot(var_df, aes(x = PC, y = Variance)) +
    geom_col(fill = "#4B9CD3") +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%%", Variance * 100)), 
              vjust = -0.3, size = 3) +
    labs(
      title = paste(domain),
      x = "Principal Component",
      y = "Proportion of Variance Explained"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )
  
 # Return PCA object and its plot
  return(list(pca = pca, plot = plot))
})

# Assign domain names to list elements
names(pca_results) <- names(domain_vars)

pca_plots <- lapply(pca_results, function(x) x$plot)
pca_grid <- wrap_plots(pca_plots) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Domain-wise PCA Results for Lothian Indicators",
    subtitle = "Each plot shows variance explained by principal components within its domain",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
  )

pca_grid

pca_domains <- c("Health", "Education", "Access")

# --- 1. Extract domain-wise PCs explaining 80% cumulative variance ---
domain_pc_list <- purrr::map(names(pca_results), function(domain) {
  
  # Skip domains not in target list
  if (!(domain %in% pca_domains)) return(NULL)
  
  res <- pca_results[[domain]]
  
  # Safety checks
  if (is.null(res) || !"pca" %in% names(res)) return(NULL)
  pca <- res$pca
  if (is.null(pca$x)) return(NULL)
  
  # Compute variance explained
  var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)
  cum_var <- cumsum(var_explained)
  
  # Retain PCs until cumulative variance >= 80%
  keep_pcs <- which(cum_var >= 0.80)[1]   # first PC index that reaches 80%
  keep_pcs <- 1:keep_pcs                  # retain all previous ones
  
  # Extract retained PC scores
  pc_data <- as.data.frame(pca$x[, keep_pcs, drop = FALSE])
  colnames(pc_data) <- paste0(domain, "_PC", keep_pcs)
  
  tibble(
    DataZone = simd_lothian$DataZone[seq_len(nrow(pc_data))],
    pc_data
  )
})


# --- 2. Combine domain PC datasets ---
lothian_pc <- domain_pc_list %>%
  compact() %>%
  reduce(full_join, by = "DataZone") %>%
  drop_na()

simd_lothian <- simd_lothian %>%
  left_join(lothian_pc, by = "DataZone")

# Scale including PC Cols
lothian_data <- simd_lothian %>%
  select(-DataZone) %>%
  scale() %>%
  as.data.frame()

pca_cols <- colnames(lothian_pc)[colnames(lothian_pc) != "DataZone"]
```

```{r results='asis'}
loading_table_list <- purrr::map(pca_domains, function(domain) {
  
  res <- pca_results[[domain]]
  if (is.null(res) || !"pca" %in% names(res)) return(NULL)
  
  pca <- res$pca
  
  # Variance explained
  var_explained <- pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)
  cum_var <- cumsum(var_explained)
  
  # Retain PCs (ensure at least PC1 is kept)
  keep_pcs <- which(cum_var >= 0.80)[1]
  if (is.na(keep_pcs)) keep_pcs <- 1  # fallback if 80% never reached
  keep_pcs <- 1:keep_pcs
  
  # Loadings
  loadings <- pca$rotation[, keep_pcs, drop = FALSE]
  
  # If loadings are empty → skip domain
  if (ncol(loadings) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  loadings <- tibble(
    Variable = rownames(loadings),
    as.data.frame(loadings)
  )
  
  colnames(loadings)[-1] <- paste0(domain, "_PC", keep_pcs)
  
  return(loadings)
})

names(loading_table_list) <- pca_domains

# Remove NULL or empty tables
loading_table_list <- loading_table_list %>%
  purrr::discard(~ is.null(.x) || nrow(.x) == 0 || ncol(.x) < 2)

library(kableExtra)
for (dom in names(loading_table_list)) {

  # Print heading as real Markdown
  cat("## PCA Loadings for ", dom, "\n\n", sep = "")

  # Print table
  loading_table_list[[dom]] %>%
    kable("latex", booktabs = TRUE, longtable = TRUE) %>%
    kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "repeat_header")) %>%
    print()

  cat("\n\n")
}
```

```{r}
pc_data  <- lothian_data %>%
  select(all_of(pca_cols),
         "income_rate", "employment_rate", "crime_rate",
         "nocentralheating_rate", "overcrowded_rate")

raw_ind <- indicator_domains %>%
  filter(Domain %in% c("Health", "Education", "Access")) %>%
  filter(!Indicator %in% c(pca_cols, "Attendance")) %>%
  pull(Indicator)

rate_ind <- c("income_rate", "employment_rate", "crime_rate",
         "nocentralheating_rate", "overcrowded_rate")

Health_ind <- indicator_domains %>% 
  filter(!Indicator %in% c(pca_cols)) %>%
  filter(Domain =="Health") %>%
  pull(Indicator)

Education_ind <- indicator_domains %>% 
  filter(!Indicator %in% c(pca_cols, "Attendance")) %>%
  filter(Domain =="Education") %>%
  pull(Indicator)

Access_ind <- indicator_domains %>% 
  filter(!Indicator %in% c(pca_cols)) %>%
  filter(Domain =="Access") %>%
  pull(Indicator)

raw_data <- lothian_data %>% select(all_of(c(raw_ind,rate_ind)))
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

p_wss1 <- fviz_nbclust(pc_data, kmeans, method = "wss") +
  labs(title = "PCA-based Data: WSS (Elbow Method)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p_sil1 <- fviz_nbclust(pc_data, kmeans, method = "silhouette") +
  labs(title = "PCA-based Data: Silhouette Score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p_wss2 <- fviz_nbclust(raw_data, kmeans, method = "wss") +
  labs(title = "Raw Indicators: WSS (Elbow Method)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p_sil2 <- fviz_nbclust(raw_data, kmeans, method = "silhouette") +
  labs(title = "Raw Indicators: Silhouette Score") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

(p_wss1 + p_sil1) / (p_wss2 + p_sil2)
```

```{r, fig.width = 10, fig.height = 12, dpi = 300}
set.seed(123)
k <- 2

k_pca <- kmeans(pc_data, centers = k, nstart = 25)
k_raw <- kmeans(raw_data, centers = k, nstart = 25)

lothian_data <- lothian_data %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_pca_k = k_pca$cluster,
    cluster_raw_k = k_raw$cluster
  )

lothian_data <- lothian_data %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_raw_k = align_by_overlap(cluster_pca_k, cluster_raw_k),
    cluster_pca_k = factor(cluster_pca_k),
    cluster_raw_k = factor(cluster_raw_k),
    cluster_diff_k = cluster_pca_k != cluster_raw_k
    )

simd_lothian_k <- simd_joined %>%
  filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
  left_join(
    lothian_data %>%
      mutate(DataZone = simd_lothian$DataZone) %>%
      select(DataZone, cluster_pca_k, cluster_raw_k, cluster_diff_k),
    by = "DataZone"
    )

```

```{r, echo = FALSE, fig.width=7, fig.height=9}

count_clusters <- function(cluster_vector) {
  tibble(cluster = cluster_vector) %>%
    mutate(cluster = forcats::fct_explicit_na(as.factor(cluster), 
                                              na_level = "NA")) %>%
    count(cluster, name = "n")
}

make_cluster_labels <- function(count_df) {
  setNames(
    paste0(count_df$cluster, " (n = ", count_df$n, ")"),
    count_df$cluster
  )
}

p_kmeans_pca <- plot_cluster_map(simd_lothian_k,
                                 "cluster_pca_k",
                                 "K-means Clusters (PCA-based, k = 2)")


p_kmeans_raw <- plot_cluster_map(simd_lothian_k,
                                 "cluster_raw_k",
                                 "K-means Clusters (Raw indicators, k = 2)")

p_kmeans_diff <- difference_map(
  simd_lothian_k,
  clusterA = "cluster_pca_k",
  clusterB = "cluster_raw_k",
  title = "Areas with Different K-means Assignments (PCA vs Raw)"
)


(p_kmeans_pca / p_kmeans_raw / p_kmeans_diff) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Comparison of K-means Clusters (k = 2): PCA vs Raw",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
    )
```

```{r cluster-boxplots, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
p_box_k_rate <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = rate_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_raw_k,
  title_text     = "Rate"
)

p_box_k_health <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Health_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_raw_k,
  title_text     = "Health"
)

p_box_k_Education <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Education_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_raw_k,
  title_text     = "Education"
)

p_box_k_Access <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Access_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_raw_k,
  title_text     = "Access"
)

(p_box_k_rate | p_box_k_health) / (p_box_k_Education | p_box_k_Access) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Comparison of Indicator Distributions Across K-means Clustering (k=2)",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    )
  ) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom"  # same legend position for all
  )
```

```{r}
sim_k_pca_raw <- similarity_metrics(
  (simd_lothian_k %>% drop_na())$cluster_pca_k,
  (simd_lothian_k %>% drop_na())$cluster_raw_k
)

kable(round(sim_k_pca_raw, 3),
      caption = "Similarity between PCA-based and Raw K-means clusters (k = 2)")
```

```{r}
cross_tab_k <- table(simd_lothian_k$cluster_pca_k, simd_lothian_k$cluster_raw_k)
cross_df_k <- as.data.frame(cross_tab_k)
names(cross_df_k) <- c("PCA_Cluster", "Raw_Cluster", "Count")

cross_df_k <- cross_df_k %>%
  group_by(PCA_Cluster) %>%
  mutate(Prop = Count / sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(cross_df_k, aes(x = factor(Raw_Cluster), y = factor(PCA_Cluster))) +
geom_tile(aes(fill = Prop), color = "white", linewidth = 0.7) +
geom_text(
  aes(label = paste0(Count, "\n(", percent(Prop, accuracy = 0.1), ")")),
  size = 3.8, fontface = "bold", lineheight = 0.9) +
scale_fill_gradient(
  low = "#deebf7", high = "#08306b",
  name = "Proportion", limits = c(0, 1),
  labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-tabulation of PCA and Raw K-means Clusters (Lothian)",
    subtitle = "Each tile shows overlap count and share of PCA cluster members.",
    x = "Raw-indicator Cluster (K-means)",
    y = "PCA-based Cluster (K-means)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid = element_blank()
)
```

```{r }
set.seed(123)

# --- distance matrices (Euclidean on already-scaled data) ---
dist_pca  <- dist(pc_data,  method = "euclidean")
dist_raw  <- dist(raw_data, method = "euclidean")

hc_pca <- hclust(dist_pca, method = "ward.D2")
hc_raw <- hclust(dist_raw, method = "ward.D2")

p_wss_h_pca <- fviz_nbclust(pc_data, hcut, method = "wss") +
  labs(title = "Hierarchical (PCA-based): WSS (Elbow)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

p_sil_h_pca <- fviz_nbclust(pc_data, hcut, method = "silhouette") +
  labs(title = "Hierarchical (PCA-based): Silhouette") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

p_wss_h_raw <- fviz_nbclust(raw_data, hcut, method = "wss") +
  labs(title = "Hierarchical (Raw): WSS (Elbow)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

p_sil_h_raw <- fviz_nbclust(raw_data, hcut, method = "silhouette") +
  labs(title = "Hierarchical (Raw): Silhouette") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))

(p_wss_h_pca + p_sil_h_pca) /
(p_wss_h_raw + p_sil_h_raw) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Comparison of Hierarchical Cluster Diagnostics: PCA vs Raw",
    subtitle = "Top: PCA-based | Bottom: Raw Indicators",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, hjust = 0.5)
    )
  )
```

```{r}
k <- 2
lothian_data <- lothian_data %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_pca_h = cutree(hc_pca, k = k),
    cluster_raw_h = cutree(hc_raw, k = k)
  )

lothian_data <- lothian_data %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_raw_h = align_by_overlap(lothian_data$cluster_pca_h,
                                     lothian_data$cluster_raw_h)
  )
```

```{r}
# p_dendro_pca <- fviz_dend(
#   hc_pca,
#   k = k,
#   rect = TRUE,
#   rect_fill = TRUE,
#   k_colors = cluster_colors3,       # use cluster colors
#   show_labels = FALSE,
#   horiz = TRUE,                     # horizontal orientation for PDF readability
#   main = "Dendrogram (PCA-based Indicators, k = 3)"
# ) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
#   theme(
#     plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
#     panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4)  # vertical grid lines
#   )
# 
# p_dendro_raw <- fviz_dend(
#   hc_raw,
#   k = k,
#   rect = TRUE,
#   rect_fill = TRUE,
#   k_colors = cluster_colors3,
#   show_labels = FALSE,
#   horiz = TRUE,
#   main = "Dendrogram (Raw Indicators, k = 3)"
# ) +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
#   theme(
#     plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
#     panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4)
#   )
# 
# # Combine for side-by-side comparison
# (p_dendro_pca | p_dendro_raw) +
#   plot_annotation(
#     title = "Hierarchical Clustering Dendrograms (k = 3): PCA vs Raw Indicators",
#     subtitle = "Color scheme matches spatial cluster maps for consistent interpretation",
#     theme = theme(
#       plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
#       plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5)
#     )
# )
```

```{r}
simd_lothian_h <- simd_joined %>%
  filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
  left_join(
    simd_lothian %>% select(DataZone),
    by = "DataZone"
    ) %>%
  left_join(
    lothian_data %>%
      mutate(DataZone = simd_lothian$DataZone) %>%
      select(DataZone, cluster_pca_h, cluster_raw_h),
    by = "DataZone"
    )

simd_lothian_h <- simd_lothian_h %>%
  mutate(cluster_diff_h = cluster_pca_h != cluster_raw_h)

simd_lothian_h <-  simd_lothian_h %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_pca_h = factor(cluster_pca_h),
    cluster_raw_h = factor(cluster_raw_h),
    cluster_diff_h = factor(cluster_diff_h))

p_h_pca <- plot_cluster_map(simd_lothian_h,
                            "cluster_pca_h",
                            "Hierarchical Clusters (PCA-based)")

p_h_raw <- plot_cluster_map(simd_lothian_h,
                            "cluster_raw_h",
                            "Hierarchical Clusters (Raw indicators)")

p_h_diff <- difference_map(
  simd_lothian_h,
  "cluster_pca_h",
  "cluster_raw_h",
  "Areas with Different Hierarchical Assignments (PCA vs Raw)"
)

(p_h_pca / p_h_raw / p_h_diff) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Comparison of Hierarchical Clusters: PCA vs Raw",
    theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"))
  )

```

```{r hier-boxplots}
p_box_h_rate <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = rate_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_raw_h,
  title_text     = "Rate"
)

p_box_h_health <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Health_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_raw_h,
  title_text     = "Health"
)

p_box_h_Education <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Education_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_raw_h,
  title_text     = "Education"
)

p_box_h_Access <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Access_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_raw_h,
  title_text     = "Access"
)

(p_box_h_rate | p_box_h_health) / (p_box_h_Education | p_box_h_Access) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Comparison of Indicator Distributions Across Hierarchical Clustering (k=2)",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    )
  ) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom"  # same legend position for all
  )
```

```{r hier-cross-metrics, message=FALSE}
# Cross-tab
cross_tab_h <- table(lothian_data$cluster_pca_h, lothian_data$cluster_raw_h)
cross_df_h <- as.data.frame(cross_tab_h)
names(cross_df_h) <- c("PCA_Cluster", "Raw_Cluster", "Count")

cross_df_h <- cross_df_h %>%
  group_by(PCA_Cluster) %>%
  mutate(Prop = Count / sum(Count)) %>%
  ungroup()

ggplot(cross_df_h,
       aes(x = factor(Raw_Cluster),
           y = factor(PCA_Cluster))) +
  geom_tile(aes(fill = Prop), colour = "white", linewidth = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(Count, "\n(", percent(Prop, 0.1), ")")),
            size = 3.5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#deebf7", high = "#08306b",
                      name = "Proportion",
                      labels = percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-tabulation of PCA and Raw hierarchical clusters (k = 3)",
    subtitle = "Counts and row-wise proportions of PCA clusters across Raw clusters",
    x = "Raw hierarchical cluster",
    y = "PCA hierarchical cluster"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )


sim_h_pca_raw <- similarity_metrics(
  (simd_lothian_h %>% drop_na())$cluster_pca_h,
  (simd_lothian_h %>% drop_na())$cluster_raw_h
)

kable(round(sim_h_pca_raw, 3),
      caption = "Similarity between PCA-based and Raw K-means clusters (k = 2)")
```

```{r}
library(e1071)  # for cmeans
k_values <- 1:10

# Storage
wcss_vector <- numeric(length(k_values))
silhouette_vector <- rep(NA_real_, length(k_values))   # silhouette is NA for k = 1
cluster_results <- vector("list", length(k_values))
names(cluster_results) <- as.character(k_values)
fcm_list <- vector("list", length(k_values))
names(fcm_list) <- as.character(k_values)

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Main k loop
# ---------------------------------------------------------
for (i in seq_along(k_values)) {
  k <- k_values[i]

  if (k == 1) {
    center <- colMeans(raw_data)
    wcss_vector[i] <- sum(rowSums((raw_data - center)^2))
    cluster_results[[as.character(k)]] <- rep(1, nrow(raw_data))
    silhouette_vector[i] <- NA
  } 
  
  else {
    fcm <- cmeans(
      raw_data,
      centers = k,
      m = 2,
      iter.max = 200,
      verbose = FALSE
    )
    
    fcm_list[[as.character(k)]] <- fcm
    cl <- fcm$cluster
    cluster_results[[as.character(k)]] <- cl

    # ----- Crisp WCSS (skip empty clusters) -----
    wss_k <- 0
    empty_cluster <- FALSE

    for (j in 1:k) {
      members <- raw_data[cl == j, , drop = FALSE]

      if (nrow(members) == 0) {
        empty_cluster <- TRUE
        next   # skip this cluster
      }

      center_j <- colMeans(members)
      wss_k <- wss_k + sum(rowSums((members - center_j)^2))
    }

    wcss_vector[i] <- wss_k

    # ----- Silhouette (requires ≥ 2 non-empty clusters) -----
    if (length(unique(cl)) < 2 || empty_cluster) {
      silhouette_vector[i] <- NA
    } else {
      sil <- silhouette(cl, dist(raw_data))
      silhouette_vector[i] <- mean(sil[, 3])
    }
  }
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Data frames
# ---------------------------------------------------------
elbow_df <- tibble(
  k = k_values,
  wcss = wcss_vector,
  silhouette = silhouette_vector
)

# Similarity metrics for k vs k+1 (start at k >= 2)
similarity_list <- list()

for (k in k_values[k_values >= 2 & k_values < max(k_values)]) {
  a <- cluster_results[[as.character(k)]]
  b <- cluster_results[[as.character(k + 1)]]

  sim <- similarity_metrics(a, b)
  sim$k <- k

  similarity_list[[as.character(k)]] <- sim
}

similarity_df <- bind_rows(similarity_list) %>% select(k, everything())

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Plots
# ---------------------------------------------------------

# WCSS Elbow Plot
ggplot(elbow_df, aes(k, wcss)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "WCSS Elbow Plot (Including k = 1)",
    x = "Number of clusters k",
    y = "WCSS"
  )

# Silhouette Plot (k = 1 will be NA)
ggplot(elbow_df, aes(k, silhouette)) +
  geom_line(na.rm = TRUE) +
  geom_point() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Silhouette Score (Crisp Cluster Labels)",
    x = "Number of clusters k",
    y = "Silhouette Score"
  )

# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Output
# ---------------------------------------------------------
print(elbow_df)
print(similarity_df)
```

```{r}
# Create a list to store membership matrices for each k
membership_list <- vector("list", length(k_values))
names(membership_list) <- as.character(k_values)

for (i in seq_along(k_values)) {
  k <- k_values[i]

  if (k == 1) {
    cluster_results[[as.character(k)]] <- rep(1, nrow(raw_data))
    membership_list[[as.character(k)]] <- matrix(1, nrow(raw_data), 1)
  } else {
    fcm <- cmeans(raw_data, centers = k, m = 2)
    cluster_results[[as.character(k)]] <- fcm$cluster
    membership_list[[as.character(k)]] <- fcm$membership
  }
}

summary_list <- list()

for (k in k_values) {
  cl <- cluster_results[[as.character(k)]]
  mem <- membership_list[[as.character(k)]]

  # cluster size
  cl_size <- table(cl)

  # check for empty clusters
  empty <- sum(cl_size == 0)

  summary_list[[as.character(k)]] <- tibble(
    k = k,
    empty_clusters = empty,
    avg_cluster_size = mean(cl_size),
    min_cluster_size = min(cl_size),
    max_cluster_size = max(cl_size),
  )
}

cluster_summary <- bind_rows(summary_list)

# Similarity metrics for k vs k+1 (start at k >= 2)
similarity_list <- list()

for (k in k_values[k_values >= 2 & k_values < max(k_values)]) {
  a <- cluster_results[[as.character(k)]]
  b <- cluster_results[[as.character(k + 1)]]

  sim <- similarity_metrics(a, b)
  sim$k <- k

  similarity_list[[as.character(k)]] <- sim
}

similarity_df <- bind_rows(similarity_list) %>% select(k, everything())

print(cluster_summary)
print(similarity_df)

```

```{r}
# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Compute fuzzy validity indices for each k
# ---------------------------------------------------------

compute_XB <- function(x, membership, centers, m = 2) {
  N <- nrow(x)
  k <- nrow(centers)

  # Squared distances x -> centers
  distmat <- matrix(0, N, k)
  for (j in 1:k) {
    diff <- sweep(x, 2, centers[j, ], "-")
    distmat[, j] <- rowSums(diff^2)
  }

  # Numerator: fuzzy compactness
  compactness <- sum((membership^m) * distmat)

  # Denominator: minimum squared center distance
  center_dist <- as.matrix(dist(centers))^2   # squared distances
  diag(center_dist) <- Inf
  min_center_dist2 <- min(center_dist)        # <-- already squared

  XB <- compactness / (N * min_center_dist2)
  return(XB)
}


compute_PC <- function(membership) {
  N <- nrow(membership)
  PC <- sum(membership^2) / N
  return(PC)
}

compute_PE <- function(membership) {
  N <- nrow(membership)
  PE <- -sum(membership * log(membership + 1e-12)) / N
  return(PE)
}

compute_FS <- function(x, membership, centers, m = 2) {
  N <- nrow(x)
  k <- nrow(centers)

  # Distances x -> centers
  distmat <- matrix(0, N, k)
  for (j in 1:k) {
    diff <- sweep(x, 2, centers[j, ], "-")
    distmat[, j] <- rowSums(diff^2)
  }

  compactness <- sum((membership^m) * distmat)

  global_mean <- colMeans(x)
  separation <- sum(rowSums((centers - global_mean)^2))

  FS <- compactness - separation
  return(FS)
}


# ---------------------------------------------------------
# Apply indices for all k
# ---------------------------------------------------------

validity_list <- list()

for (k in k_values[k_values >= 2]) {

  cl <- cluster_results[[as.character(k)]]
  mem <- membership_list[[as.character(k)]]
  centers <- fcm_list[[as.character(k)]]$centers
  
  XB <- compute_XB(raw_data, mem, centers)
  PC <- compute_PC(mem)
  PE <- compute_PE(mem)
  FS <- compute_FS(raw_data, mem, centers)
  
  validity_list[[as.character(k)]] <- tibble(
    k = k,
    XB = XB,
    PC = PC,
    PE = PE,
    FS = FS
  )
}

validity_df <- bind_rows(validity_list)
print(validity_df)

```

Xie–Beni (XB) – “compactness / separation” collapsed due to the small separation. 

```{r}
fcm_raw <- fcm_list[2]$"2"

# Hard cluster labels (maximum membership)
lothian_data <- lothian_data %>%
  mutate(
    cluster_fcm = fcm_raw$cluster
  )
```


```{r}
simd_lothian_fcm <- simd_joined %>%
  filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
  left_join(
    lothian_data %>%
      mutate(DataZone = simd_lothian$DataZone) %>%
      select(DataZone, cluster_fcm),
    by = "DataZone"
    ) 

p_fcm <- plot_cluster_map(simd_lothian_fcm,
                                 "cluster_fcm",
                                 "C-means Clusters (Raw indicators)")

p_fcm
```

```{r}
p_box_fcm_rate <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = rate_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_fcm,
  title_text     = "Rate"
)

p_box_fcm_health <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Health_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_fcm,
  title_text     = "Health"
)

p_box_fcm_Education <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Education_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_fcm,
  title_text     = "Education"
)

p_box_fcm_Access <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
  data          = lothian_data,
  indicator_cols = Access_ind,
  cluster_col    = cluster_fcm,
  title_text     = "Access"
)

(p_box_fcm_rate | p_box_fcm_health) / (p_box_fcm_Education | p_box_fcm_Access) +
  plot_annotation(
    title = "Comparison of Indicator Distributions Across Fuzzy C-means (k=2)",
    theme = theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    )
  ) &
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom"  # same legend position for all
  )
```

```{r}
# k_values <- 1:10
# 
# fmm_list <- list()
# 
# bic <- numeric(length(k_values))
# 
# icl <- numeric(length(k_values))
# 
# set.seed(123)
# 
# similarity_df <- bind_rows(similarity_list) %>% select(k, everything())
# 
# for (i in seq_along(k_values)) {
#   k <- k_values[i]
#   
#   fmm <- Mclust(raw_data, G = k)
#   
#   fmm_list[[as.character(k)]] <- fmm
#   
#   bic[i] <- fmm$bic
#   
#   icl[i] <- fmm$icl
#   
# }
```

```{r}
# fm_scores <- tibble(
#   k = k_values,
#   BIC = bic,
#   ICL = icl
# )
# 
# print(fm_scores)
# 
# fm_scores_long <- fm_scores %>%
#   pivot_longer(cols = -k, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")
# 
# ggplot(fm_scores_long, aes(k, value, color = metric)) +
#   geom_line() +
#   geom_point() +
#   theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
#   labs(
#     title = "Model Selection for Gaussian Mixture Models",
#     x = "Number of Clusters (k)",
#     y = "Score (Higher is better)"
#   )
```

```{r fmm-fit, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# set.seed(123)
# 
# k = 2
# 
# fmm <- fmm_list[k]$'2'
# 
# lothian_data <- lothian_data %>%
#   mutate(
#     cluster_fmm = fmm$classification
#   )
# 
# # Align RAW FMM clusters to PCA FMM clusters
# lothian_data <- lothian_data %>%
#   mutate(
#     cluster_fmm = factor(cluster_fmm, levels = 1:k)
#   )
```

```{r fmm-maps, fig.width=7, fig.height=9}
# simd_lothian_fmm <- simd_joined %>%
#   filter(Joint_Board_area == "Lothian") %>%
#   left_join(
#     lothian_data %>%
#       mutate(DataZone = simd_lothian$DataZone) %>%
#       select(DataZone, cluster_fmm),
#     by = "DataZone"
#   )
# 
# p_fmm_raw <- plot_cluster_map(simd_lothian_fmm,
#                               "cluster_fmm",
#                               "FMM Clusters (Raw indicators)")
# 
# p_fmm_raw
```

```{r fmm-boxplots}
# p_box_fmm_rate <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
#   data          = lothian_data,
#   indicator_cols = rate_ind,
#   cluster_col    = cluster_fmm,
#   title_text     = "Rate"
# )
# 
# p_box_fmm_health <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
#   data          = lothian_data,
#   indicator_cols = Health_ind,
#   cluster_col    = cluster_fmm,
#   title_text     = "Health"
# )
# 
# p_box_fmm_Education <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
#   data          = lothian_data,
#   indicator_cols = Education_ind,
#   cluster_col    = cluster_fmm,
#   title_text     = "Education"
# )
# 
# p_box_fmm_Access <- plot_cluster_boxplot(
#   data          = lothian_data,
#   indicator_cols = Access_ind,
#   cluster_col    = cluster_fmm,
#   title_text     = "Access"
# )
# 
# (p_box_fmm_rate | p_box_fmm_health) / (p_box_fmm_Education | p_box_fmm_Access) +
#   plot_annotation(
#     title = "Comparison of Indicator Distributions Across FMM (k=2)",
#     theme = theme(
#       plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
#     )
#   ) &
#   theme(
#     legend.position = "bottom"  # same legend position for all
#   )
```

```{r}
# for (k in k_values[k_values >= 2 & k_values < max(k_values)]) {
#   a <- cluster_results[[as.character(k)]]
#   b <- cluster_results[[as.character(k + 1)]]
# 
#   sim <- similarity_metrics(a, b)
#   sim$k <- k
# 
#   similarity_list[[as.character(k)]] <- sim
# }
# 
# similarity_df <- bind_rows(similarity_list) %>% select(k, everything())

```

## Vine-Copula
```{r}
sim_k_h <- similarity_metrics(
  (simd_lothian_k %>% drop_na())$cluster_raw_k,
  (simd_lothian_h %>% drop_na())$cluster_raw_h
)

kable(round(sim_k_h, 3),
      caption = "Similarity between Hierechical and K-means clusters (k = 2)")

sim_k_fcm <- similarity_metrics(
  (simd_lothian_k %>% drop_na())$cluster_raw_k,
  (simd_lothian_fcm %>% drop_na())$cluster_fcm
)

kable(round(sim_k_fcm, 3),
      caption = "Similarity between c-means and K-means clusters (k = 2)")
```
